channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Last Year’s Best Dress Watch Gets a Sequel That’s Even Better - Gear Patrol
item.description:
1. Last Year’s Best Dress Watch Gets a Sequel That’s Even Better  Gear Patrol

item.title: Nintendo Breaks Silence on New Super Mario Game - comicbook.com
item.description:
1. Nintendo Breaks Silence on New Super Mario Game  comicbook.com

  4. Far cry from 16-pixel start, Mario makes it 'so big' on screen: creator Miyamoto  Edition.mv
  5. Shigeru Miyamoto said he’s really looking forward to the next Mario game  My Nintendo News

item.title: This wearable $2,000 AI exoskeleton wants to turn your legs into hybrid hardware - TechSpot
item.description:
1. This wearable $2,000 AI exoskeleton wants to turn your legs into hybrid hardware  TechSpot
  2. E-Hiking Is Here. You Can Tell by My 1,000-Watt Hips.  WSJ
  3. Can Using the Hypershell Exoskeleton on a Bike Replace an E-Bike? I Tested It to Find Out  CNET
  4. I Tried the Newest Exoskeleton for Hikers. It Could Help Millions Enjoy the Trail.  Outside Magazine
  5. Hypershell unveils X Series exoskeletons with a huge AI and speed injection  Wareable

item.title: This $82 USB cable hides a Wi-Fi microcontroller inside its connector - TechSpot
item.description:
1. This $82 USB cable hides a Wi-Fi microcontroller inside its connector  TechSpot
  2. Wi-Fi controlled hacking USB cable stealthily packs in a microcontroller, microSD storage, and more — cable executes remote payload execution, keystroke injection, and more, but is 'built for makers, developers, enthusiasts, and cybersecurity learners'  Tom's Hardware
  3. This “normal” USB cable secretly wants to be a hacking tool  Digital Trends
  4. Hacknect: A Wireless Automation Platform Inside a USB Cable  Hackster.io
  5. Hacknect – A wireless hacking USB cable with a built-in microSD card slot (Crowdfunding)  CNX Software

item.title: Linus Torvalds to ‘start being more hardnosed’ about ‘pointless pull requests’ – some of which come from AIs - The Register
item.description:
1. Linus Torvalds to ‘start being more hardnosed’ about ‘pointless pull requests’ – some of which come from AIs  The Register
  2. AI is drowning software maintainers in junk security reports  Help Net Security
  3. Linus says Linux's trivial fixes are getting out of hand, and AI reviewers are making it worse  XDA
  4. Linus Torvalds says Linux security list is becoming ‘unmanageable’ due to AI bug reports  The Verge
  5. Linus Torvalds on the AI claim that makes him angry, and what security researchers should never do  ZDNET
